title: In A Gadda Da Leela

jokes.text: I wax my rocket every day!
jokes.setup: Advertisement for rocket wax product
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo through double meaning of 'rocket' and 'wax'
jokes.explanation: Simple sexual innuendo that's economical but relies on cheap double entendre

jokes.text: Well, obviously, Zapp Brannigan could save you, but...
jokes.setup: Leela asks 'What about Zapp Brannigan?' as if he's not there
jokes.punchline: Zapp responds in third person acknowledging his heroic status while completely missing the irony
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp character moment - his oblivious narcissism displayed through third-person self-aggrandizement

jokes.text: I surrender and volunteer for treason.
jokes.setup: Romantic scene between Zapp and Leela
jokes.punchline: Zapp immediately abandons his heroic role at first sign of pleasure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific absurdist moment - only Zapp would 'volunteer for treason' in this context

jokes.text: The long, dramatic corridor. That's never a good sign.
jokes.setup: Walking down a dramatic government corridor
jokes.punchline: Zapp recognizes the ominous nature of the cinematic trope
jokes.explanation: Mild meta-humor acknowledging storytelling conventions

jokes.text: Glove recognized. Proceed, Mrs. Eisenhower.
jokes.setup: High-tech security system scanning for authorization
jokes.punchline: System identifies Nixon as Mrs. Eisenhower based on glove scan
jokes.explanation: Absurd technology failure with historical reference humor

jokes.text: Reptilicus, hit the thingy.
jokes.setup: Presidential briefing requiring technical presentation
jokes.punchline: Nixon uses informal 'thingy' for sophisticated equipment and calls aide 'Reptilicus'
jokes.explanation: Character-specific humor showing Nixon's casual approach to formal situations

jokes.text: That's all the resolution we have. Making it bigger doesn't make it clearer. It does on CSI: Miami.
jokes.setup: Why the death-sphere image is still blurry when magnified
jokes.punchline: Nixon's misunderstanding of how digital enhancement works, referencing TV trope
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about TV trope vs reality

jokes.text: but it was like shooting bbs at Bebe Rebozo.
jokes.setup: Describing the futility of fighting the death-sphere
jokes.punchline: Nixon uses obscure reference to his real-life friend Bebe Rebozo
jokes.explanation: Deep-cut historical reference that rewards informed viewers

jokes.text: Erased that planet like 18 minutes of incriminating tape.
jokes.setup: Describing how thoroughly the death-sphere destroyed a planet
jokes.punchline: Nixon compares it to his own Watergate scandal's missing tape minutes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Nixon casually referencing his own scandal while describing destruction

jokes.text: Oh, I just wish I understood why. Why I should care.
jokes.setup: Zapp seemingly shows concern about the planet's destruction
jokes.punchline: He's actually just wondering why he should care about it
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection showcasing Zapp's selfishness disguised as philosophical pondering

jokes.text: My instinct is to hide in this barrel like the wily fish.
jokes.setup: Following his 'fish in a barrel' metaphor
jokes.punchline: Zapp misunderstands the idiom and thinks fish choose to be in barrels as strategy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific misunderstanding that reveals Zapp's backwards military thinking

jokes.text: Let's see how crazy I am now, Nixon. The correct answer is 'very.'
jokes.setup: Professor challenges Nixon's dismissal of him as crazy
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits he is indeed very crazy
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment from Professor acknowledging his own mental state

jokes.text: It's right in front of you. I find that... Ow! ...To believe.
jokes.setup: Professor demonstrates ship's invisibility
jokes.punchline: Zapp walks into invisible ship mid-sentence, interrupting his skeptical response
jokes.explanation: Well-timed physical comedy that proves the point while punishing skepticism

jokes.text: But how exactly is this happy meal toy going to destroy a giant death-sphere?
jokes.setup: Questioning the tiny ship's effectiveness
jokes.punchline: Dismissive comparison to children's toy
jokes.explanation: Solid insult that captures the visual absurdity through Zapp's crude analogy

jokes.text: All death-spheres have one vulnerable opening.
jokes.setup: Question about vulnerable opening
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact statement about sci-fi trope
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor that acknowledges the Death Star trope as if it's a law of physics

jokes.text: I say Brannigan. I say no. - I say me. - I say Leela. - I say yes. - I say no. I say Leela, too. I say yes again.
jokes.setup: Debate over who should pilot the dangerous mission
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire 'I say' repetition building to absurd speed
jokes.explanation: Verbal comedy through repetitive structure and escalating pace

jokes.text: It'll help us achieve maximum thrust.
jokes.setup: Leela asks why she has to sit in Zapp's lap
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo disguised as technical jargon
jokes.explanation: Typical Zapp sexual innuendo, predictable but in character

jokes.text: I haven't felt much of anything since my Guinea pig died.
jokes.setup: Fry asking if anyone else feels aroused, jealous, and worried
jokes.punchline: Bender's unexpectedly vulnerable confession about pet death
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly touching moment from Bender that subverts his usual callousness

jokes.text: Feel the entrance, Leela.
jokes.setup: GPS navigation system providing directions
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo in guidance to death-sphere entrance
jokes.explanation: Cheap sexual innuendo without much craft

jokes.text: V-giny? Doesn't ring a bell. Hmm, I don't like the looks of this V-giny.
jokes.setup: Characters trying to decipher mysterious letters on death-sphere
jokes.punchline: Letters spell obvious sexual reference that they don't recognize
jokes.explanation: Obvious innuendo joke but the characters' obliviousness adds a layer

jokes.text: This is it... the moment we should have trained for.
jokes.setup: Critical moment requiring skill and preparation
jokes.punchline: Zapp admits they didn't actually train for this moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of military incompetence through casual admission of unpreparedness

jokes.text: I can't face this mysterious planet alone without you or someone like you.
jokes.setup: Romantic declaration to unconscious Leela
jokes.punchline: Zapp's attempt at romance includes escape clause 'or someone like you'
jokes.explanation: Undermines romantic moment with typical Zapp self-serving qualifier

jokes.text: If anyone can move it, I can. No one can move it.
jokes.setup: Zapp boasts about his strength to help Leela
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits defeat after trying
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy in showing Zapp's bravado immediately deflated by reality

jokes.text: This space suit is making me sweat like a sow.
jokes.setup: Leela complaining about discomfort in space suit
jokes.punchline: Unflattering comparison to female pig
jokes.explanation: Simple gross-out humor without much craft

jokes.text: I'll try to avert my eyes from your nudery.
jokes.setup: Zapp promising to be respectful while they undress
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'nudery' and obvious insincerity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with invented word that sounds both formal and ridiculous

jokes.text: You will? Eh, what's the point? You'll have to look sooner or later.
jokes.setup: Leela surprised by Zapp's apparent decency
jokes.punchline: Zapp immediately reveals his ulterior logic
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp misdirection - appearing noble before revealing selfish motivation

jokes.text: What a thoughtful and considerate thing to say. What the hell's wrong with you?
jokes.setup: Zapp being uncharacteristically considerate
jokes.punchline: Leela's suspicious reaction to his unusual decency
jokes.explanation: Great character-aware moment - Leela knows Zapp well enough to be suspicious of kindness

jokes.text: Perhaps it's the fresh air or a severe head injury.
jokes.setup: Zapp wondering why he's being considerate
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of potential brain damage as explanation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor treating serious injury casually, typical of Zapp's self-awareness

jokes.text: How should I know? And how come you never ask if I'm okay? I'm feeling a little neglected here.
jokes.setup: Fry asking if Bender thinks Leela is okay
jokes.punchline: Bender makes the concern about himself instead
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender selfishness turning others' crises into personal grievances

jokes.text: Well, I guess it's time to indulge in some end-of-the-world debauchery. Who's up for an orgy?
jokes.setup: Bender suggesting end-times activities
jokes.punchline: Immediately escalates to most extreme suggestion
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender excess, though the escalation is somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Maybe a parcheesi tournament.
jokes.setup: Bender's orgy suggestion
jokes.punchline: Amy's wholesome alternative to debauchery
jokes.explanation: Good contrast humor - innocent board game vs. orgy

jokes.text: Like The Pimpsons and Ass-o-rama?
jokes.setup: Professor mentioning censored TV programs
jokes.punchline: Crude parodies of real show names
jokes.explanation: Simple crude wordplay without much sophistication

jokes.text: First came X-3 the nude beach planet, then Poopiter, and finally that world that can't be mentioned in polite company.
jokes.setup: Listing planets destroyed by censorship satellite
jokes.punchline: Escalating indecency from nude beaches to 'Poopiter' to unmentionable
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with juvenile humor that serves the plot

jokes.text: You mean... How dare you.
jokes.setup: Professor mentions unmentionable planet name
jokes.punchline: Fry starts to say it, Leela's shocked reaction
jokes.explanation: Implied humor where audience imagination fills in offensive planet name

jokes.text: Why couldn't a water fountain have fallen on me?
jokes.setup: Zapp trapped and thirsty under a log
jokes.punchline: Wishes for equally improbable but more useful accident
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor showing Zapp's illogical problem-solving approach

jokes.text: Stealth technology makes the ship impossible to locate, yet easy to stub your crotch on.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they can't be rescued
jokes.punchline: Specific physical comedy consequence of invisibility
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about unintended consequences of technology

jokes.text: It's a veritable garden of eden. It is?
jokes.setup: Zapp describing their paradise-like situation
jokes.punchline: Leela's confused reaction to his optimism
jokes.explanation: Sets up Biblical parallels that will pay off later

jokes.text: No. We're both delirious from dehydration. Just like Adam and Eve.
jokes.setup: Snake appears to say hello
jokes.punchline: Zapp's confused Biblical reference
jokes.explanation: Zapp's garbled understanding of Biblical stories creates mild absurdist humor

jokes.text: I'll thinks it overs while I engages is five-dollar hooker.
jokes.setup: Urgent appeal to avoid sin to save Earth
jokes.punchline: Scruffy's casual approach to prostitution while considering the message
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy character moment with his distinctive speech pattern and priorities

jokes.text: Yuck! I don't wants no three-dollar hooker. I'm going backs to the adults bookstore.
jokes.setup: Price negotiation for prostitution
jokes.punchline: Scruffy has standards but still chooses adult bookstore
jokes.explanation: Continues Scruffy's character consistency while escalating the adult content theme

jokes.text: Adult bookstore? I thought this was the public library. No. Pubic library.
jokes.setup: Fry's innocent mistake about location
jokes.punchline: Clerk's correction reveals crude wordplay
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay joke that advances the episode's theme about indecency

jokes.text: Oh, God, I'm actually starting to miss him. Maybe you two belong together. We do not. And you can't talk.
jokes.setup: Leela missing Zapp, snake offering relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Leela's defensive reaction to snake's observation
jokes.explanation: Absurdist humor with talking snake providing relationship counseling

jokes.text: Guess who just killed a woodchuck with his bare feet?
jokes.setup: Zapp returning from hunting
jokes.punchline: Bizarre boast about using feet instead of hands for killing
jokes.explanation: Absurdist specificity - using feet instead of hands creates unexpected visual

jokes.text: Well, now I've heard everything. On my iPod.
jokes.setup: Professor's outlandish plan
jokes.punchline: Hermes' comment is actually about his iPod, not the plan
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - audience assumes reaction to plan but it's unrelated

jokes.text: Hey, I'm proud of you, Bender... avoiding sin for almost a full minute. Bender?
jokes.setup: Fry praising Bender's brief purity
jokes.punchline: Realization that Bender has already abandoned purity
jokes.explanation: Good timing comedy showing Bender's predictable inability to resist temptation

jokes.text: What's the worst that could happen?
jokes.setup: Bender engaging in sinful behavior while Earth is threatened
jokes.punchline: Classic jinx line that guarantees disaster
jokes.explanation: Classic setup line that every viewer knows will lead to immediate consequences

jokes.text: We're the only two humans left in the universe!
jokes.setup: Earth apparently destroyed
jokes.punchline: Zapp's romantic opportunity from humanity's extinction
jokes.explanation: Dark humor turning global catastrophe into personal romantic opportunity

jokes.text: Like the tuba. Yes! We'll be like Adam and Eve. Only without the tuba.
jokes.setup: Planning to avoid humanity's mistakes
jokes.punchline: Arbitrary focus on tuba as humanity's key error
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor - of all humanity's mistakes, they fixate on the tuba

jokes.text: Yuck! Is that really what happened in the Bible? It's a sick and twisted book of holiness, all right.
jokes.setup: Realizing implications of Adam and Eve story
jokes.punchline: Zapp's assessment of the Bible as 'sick and twisted book of holiness'
jokes.explanation: Subversive religious humor with Zapp's contradictory phrasing

jokes.text: With less Sodom and more Gomorrah.
jokes.setup: Planning to write their own Bible
jokes.punchline: Zapp's confused preference between equally sinful cities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp moment - confident declaration that makes no logical sense

jokes.text: Those nut-berries you found were so salty, I couldn't think straight. I mean, not to belabor it, but they were as salty as that bag of trail mix Fry gave me. Exactly as salty.
jokes.setup: Leela commenting on the salty nuts
jokes.punchline: Her dawning realization through repetitive emphasis
jokes.explanation: Good structure showing character's gradual realization building to revelation

jokes.text: I exaggerated when I said I picked it from the treetops.
jokes.setup: Confession about the food source
jokes.punchline: 'Exaggerated' is massive understatement for complete fabrication
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment where Zapp minimizes his massive deception with typical understatement

jokes.text: I wanted to offer you the chocolates... I truly did... but I was concerned about your waistline. Very concerned.
jokes.setup: Zapp explaining why he hoarded food while she suffered
jokes.punchline: His 'thoughtful' concern for her weight as justification
jokes.explanation: Peak Zapp awfulness - turning selfishness into fake concern for her benefit

jokes.text: But then I wouldn't have been able to provide the loving care you wouldn't have needed.
jokes.setup: Zapp admitting he could have freed her from the log
jokes.punchline: Circular logic about providing unnecessary care
jokes.explanation: Brilliant twisted logic that perfectly captures Zapp's self-serving rationalizations

jokes.text: Ah, this seems like a good place to take a dump.
jokes.setup: Emotional reunion scene between Leela and Fry
jokes.punchline: Bender's crude interruption of tender moment
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender moment ruining emotional scenes with crude practicality

jokes.text: You said there wasn't any toilet paper! And that humanity was annihilated!
jokes.setup: Leela's outrage at deception
jokes.punchline: Equates lack of toilet paper with genocide
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Leela's practical concerns alongside cosmic ones

jokes.text: The alleged 'telescope' was a toilet paper tube. You said there wasn't any toilet paper!
jokes.setup: Zapp revealing his deception about Earth's destruction
jokes.punchline: Leela catches him in another lie about toilet paper
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows the web of Zapp's lies unraveling

jokes.text: Hear the righteous word of the mighty V-giny! The mighty V-giny!
jokes.setup: Censorship satellite addressing Earth
jokes.punchline: Self-referential proclamation of its own name's sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Absurd moment where censorship device proudly proclaims sexually suggestive name

jokes.text: Unacceptable wardrobe malfunction!
jokes.setup: Adam and Eve situation with leaf coverings
jokes.punchline: Reference to Janet Jackson Super Bowl incident
jokes.explanation: Topical reference to real censorship controversy

jokes.text: Well... if it's to save Earth... I guess I could take one for the team.
jokes.setup: Satellite demanding they consummate their union
jokes.punchline: Zapp frames sex with Leela as a noble sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp line presenting his desires as heroic sacrifice

jokes.text: Those giant guns are making me feel sort of inadequate. I'm not sure if I can...
jokes.setup: Satellite's threatening weapons pointed at them
jokes.punchline: Zapp's performance anxiety from weapon size comparison
jokes.explanation: Character-specific insecurity manifesting at the worst possible moment

jokes.text: Move it. I gotta get home and do laundry.
jokes.setup: Global crisis requiring intimate performance to save world
jokes.punchline: Bender's mundane domestic concern taking priority
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan moment - laundry concerns trump saving humanity

jokes.text: Was it as good for you as it was for the human race?
jokes.setup: After saving Earth through their union
jokes.punchline: Zapp's post-coital question expanding scope to all humanity
jokes.explanation: Excellent closing line that ties personal intimacy to global salvation with typical Zapp ego

tmdb_overview: Leela and Zapp Brannigan find themselves stranded on an Eden-like planet.
